label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's figure out which of these two statements is sarcastic. First, I need to understand what sarcasm is. Sarcasm is a form of irony in which one says the opposite of what one is really feeling. It's often used to mock or convey contempt. So the key is that the speaker is saying something, but their real meaning is the opposite. Let me analyze each statement. Statement (A): "Stop putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea" This is saying "stop doing X — good idea" where X is "putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point." The phrase "putting people in neat little compartments" likely means labeling or categorizing people into groups, possibly in a simplistic or reductive way, to protect one's own opinion or to avoid having to engage with more
